commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands shared and embedded assistant-text sanitization so `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks are stripped from chat replies, with focused sanitizer, partitioner, extraction, and subscriber tests.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +189. Total +287 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-head regression: the live check-run annotation shows `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` failing because literal `<reasoning>` prose is truncated. I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can target the partitioner’s visible-mode recovery logic and update focused coverage without a product or security decision.

target.title: fix(agents): strip reasoning tags from chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in the shared final assistant text sanitizer.
- Align the embedded stream/block-reply tag matcher, message recompute trigger, and stream partitioner with the same exact tag-name boundary so visible XML-ish tags such as `<reasoning-step>` and `<antml:reasoning.step>` stay visible.
- Treat self-closing reasoning tags such as `<reasoning/>` as empty hidden-control blocks so they do not hide or stream the following visible text.
- Keep unclosed `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks hidden in visible-mode stream partitioning, matching the shared sanitizer while preserving legacy `<think>` recovery.
- Add focused sanitizer, partitioner, embedded extraction, and subscriber regressions for regular, namespaced, split, attribute-bearing, visible-prefix, self-closing, and unclosed tags.

## Root Cause

The OpenAI-compatible stream partitioner already recognized `reasoning` tags, but the final sanitizer and embedded stream delivery matchers only covered think/thinking/thought variants. That left `<reasoning>...</reasoning>` text able to survive final assistant extraction or chat block-reply delivery. Split opener fragments could leak when the stream buffered only the older tag names, and attribute-bearing openers could still be missed by the embedded stream scanner even though the shared sanitizer accepted attributes.

The first attribute-support patch used `\b[^<>]*>` as the tag tail. That accepted visible tag names that merely started with `reasoning`, such as `<reasoning-step>`, because `\b` matches before `-` and `.`. The current patch now requires the next character after the recognized control tag name to be whitespace, `/`, or `>`.

Self-closing open tags were also being treated as normal openers. For `<reasoning/>Visible`, that could hide the visible suffix in text sanitizers or stream `Visible` as reasoning text in embedded reasoning extraction.

The stream partitioner's visible-mode unclosed-tag recovery was intentionally permissive for legacy `<think>` output. After adding `reasoning` to the same partitioner grammar, that recovery also made unclosed `<reasoning>secret` visible. The current patch gates that recovery so `reasoning` and `antml:reasoning` keep the stricter hidden behavior used by the shared sanitizer.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Assistant-visible final text and embedded chat block-reply streaming now strip hidden `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks through the same reasoning-tag taxonomy used for existing think/thinking tags, including when opening tags are split across stream chunks, carry attributes, are self-closing, or end unclosed. Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` remain user-visible.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `890ae0447cadd3aaf6b9c37489d13f72ed1a3888`, running the source shared sanitizer, embedded assistant extraction, embedded reasoning stream extraction, stream partitioner, and focused `subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession` block-reply regression tests with `blockReplyBreak: "text_end"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` source probes that imported `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts`, and `src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts`; they exercised `<reasoning/>Visible`, `<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>`, `<reasoning>secret`, `Visible <reasoning>secret`, and `Visible <antml:reasoning>secret`. Also ran the focused Vitest files covering shared sanitizer behavior, the stream partitioner, embedded extraction/reasoning stream behavior, split/attribute-bearing block replies, visible `reasoning-step` block replies, self-closing block replies, and unclosed visible-mode partitioner recovery.

Evidence after fix: Console output from direct source probes:

```json
{
  "sanitizerSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "sanitizerPrefix": "<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>",
  "visibleExtractionSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "reasoningStreamSelfClosing": "",
  "partitionerSelfClosing": [
    {
      "kind": "text",
      "text": "Visible"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{"input":"<reasoning>secret","deltas":[{"kind":"thinking","text":"secret"}],"visibleTextDeltas":[],"sanitizer":""}
{"input":"Visible <reasoning>secret","deltas":[{"kind":"text","text":"Visible "},{"kind":"thinking","text":"secret"}],"visibleTextDeltas":["Visible "],"sanitizer":"Visible"}
{"input":"Visible <antml:reasoning>secret","deltas":[{"kind":"text","text":"Visible "},{"kind":"thinking","text":"secret"}],"visibleTextDeltas":["Visible "],"sanitizer":"Visible"}
```

Observed result after fix: The visible suffix after `<reasoning/>` remains visible, self-closing tags emit no reasoning stream text, visible `reasoning-step` content is preserved, and unclosed `reasoning`/`antml:reasoning` blocks produce hidden partitioner deltas rather than recovered visible text. Focused subscriber tests also show attribute-bearing reasoning blocks are withheld until the close tag arrives and only the visible final reply is emitted.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, broad changed checks, GitHub CI beyond the checks GitHub ran for this PR, and live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec tsx --eval '...'` direct source proofs above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files / 239 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
